title: mammillaria elongata var. stella-aurata
intro: Mammillaria elongata var. stella-aurata, also known as the Gold Star Pincushion Cactus, is a rare and beautiful species of cactus in the mammillaria genus, characterized by its long, thin stems covered in fine, golden spines. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine in Mexico, where it is believed to possess medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Mammillaria elongata var. stella-aurata include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the stems in water or alcohol, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
